techniques.evidence_quote: through those services, discovered a misconfigured firewall in the Capital One AWS environment
A firewall misconfiguration permitted commands to reach and be executed by that server, which enabled access to folders or buckets
the attacker used Tor and IPredator in combination to obfuscate her identity
curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/... the first command, when executed, obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role

aws iam list-roles, aws iam list-policies, aws iam list-instance-profiles... These do what it says on the tin — enumerate the catalogue of available IAM resources an attacker might want to use once they have shell on an EC2 instance.
the April 21 File contained code for three commands, as well as a list of more than 700 folders or buckets of data

the third command (the 'Sync Command'), when executed, used the ***-WAF-Role to extract or copy data from those folders or buckets in Capital One's storage space for which the ***-WAF-Role account had the requisite permissions.

attack_chain.description: Attacker used Tor and IPredator to anonymize their identity while scanning Capital One's AWS environment, discovering a misconfigured firewall/Security Group with a dangerous port exposed to the internet.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: through those services, discovered a misconfigured firewall in the Capital One AWS environment

attack_chain.description: Attacker routed all attack traffic through Tor combined with IPredator VPN to obfuscate their true origin and identity throughout the attack.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker used Tor and IPredator in combination to obfuscate her identity

attack_chain.description: Attacker exploited the misconfigured firewall (WAF/Security Group) to reach and execute commands on the EC2 server — a Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) condition that allowed interaction with the EC2 instance metadata service.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: A firewall misconfiguration permitted commands to reach and be executed by that server, which enabled access to folders or buckets

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: Attacker queried the EC2 Instance Metadata Service (IMDS) at 169.254.169.254 to retrieve the temporary IAM security credentials (Access Key, Secret Key, Session Token) associated with the WAF-Role attached to the EC2 instance.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: curl http://169.254.169.254/latest/meta-data/iam/security-credentials/... the first command, when executed, obtained the security credentials for an account known as ***-WAF-Role

attack_chain.description: Using the stolen IAM credentials, attacker enumerated available IAM roles, policies, and instance profiles to identify roles with broader S3 permissions, and potentially used 'aws ec2 replace-iam-instance-profile-association' to assume a more privileged role.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: aws iam list-roles, aws iam list-policies, aws iam list-instance-profiles... These do what it says on the tin — enumerate the catalogue of available IAM resources an attacker might want to use once they have shell on an EC2 instance.

attack_chain.description: Using the over-privileged WAF-Role credentials, attacker enumerated over 700 S3 buckets and folders accessible to the role, generating a target list for exfiltration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the April 21 File contained code for three commands, as well as a list of more than 700 folders or buckets of data

attack_chain.description: Attacker executed 'aws s3 sync' (the Sync Command) using the WAF-Role credentials to extract and copy sensitive data — including over 100 million credit card applications — from Capital One's private S3 buckets.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the third command (the 'Sync Command'), when executed, used the ***-WAF-Role to extract or copy data from those folders or buckets in Capital One's storage space for which the ***-WAF-Role account had the requisite permissions.

initial_access_vector: Misconfigured WAF/Security Group exposed a dangerous port to the internet, enabling SSRF-style commands to be executed on the EC2 server
privilege_escalation: Over-privileged EC2 IAM role (***-WAF-Role) with broad S3 read/list permissions; potential additional escalation via 'aws ec2 replace-iam-instance-profile-association' to swap in a more privileged IAM instance profile
